steps.prompt: Which word is the verb in the conditional clause?

hints.increpa: 'Increpa' is the main clause verb — an imperative meaning 'rebuke'
hints.illum: 'Illum' is a demonstrative pronoun meaning 'him' in the accusative

steps.reveal: peccaverit = will have sinned (if he shall have sinned)

hints.perfect subjunctive: The form is identical — but with imperative 'increpa' in the apodosis, future perfect fits the conditional
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect indicative would be 'peccavit' — 'peccaverit' has the -eri- formation
hints.pluperfect subjunctive: Pluperfect subjunctive would be 'peccavisset' — 'peccaverit' is future perfect

hints.pecto, pectere, pexi, pexum: These are the parts of 'pectere' (to comb)
hints.pango, pangere, pepigi, pactum: These are the parts of 'pangere' (to fix/agree)
hints.percutio, percutere, percussi, percussum: These are the parts of 'percutere' (to strike)

hints.present indicative: Present indicative 3rd sg. would be 'increpat' — 'increpa' is a command
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'increpet' — 'increpa' is a direct imperative
hints.infinitive: Infinitive would be 'increpare' — 'increpa' is the singular imperative

hints.crepo, crepare, crepui, crepitum: These are the parts of 'crepare' (to rattle/crack) — the simple verb
hints.crepo, crepare, crepavi, crepatum: The simple 'crepare' has 'crepui' not 'crepavi' — 'increpare' regularizes to -avi
hints.incido, incidere, incidi, incasum: These are the parts of 'incidere' (to fall upon)

hints.vocative: Vocative would also be 'frater' but here it is the subject, not being addressed

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'fratris' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'fratris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'fratris' ends in -is

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'ille' — 'illum' with -um is accusative

translation: If your brother sins, rebuke him
